acceptance.rule: has_axis (cmplog, value_profile_cmplog) >= lacks_axis (naive, value_profile) at every chain length; naive >> 0 at LITERAL_CHAIN_LEN=1 (single 4-byte gate occasionally landed), naive=0 while cmplog>0 at LITERAL_CHAIN_LEN>=2; separation grows monotonically with chain length

design_basis: Each chain step is one opaque 4-byte exact-literal gate that must clear before the next is reachable; the trap fires only on full-chain success. I2S substitutes each logged literal operand one gate at a time so cmplog/vpc time-to-clear scales ~linearly in chain length, while blind search must land all 4*LITERAL_CHAIN_LEN literal bytes by chance (~1/256^(4*N) collapse). Increasing LITERAL_CHAIN_LEN therefore widens the I2S-vs-blind-search advantage with no partial-match ladder offered to naive.
notes: LITERAL_CHAIN_LEN=1: single 4-byte literal gate (1/256^4 per exec for blind). cmplog/vpc resolve fast; naive may land a handful in 600s, value_profile likewise.
LITERAL_CHAIN_LEN=2: two sequential 4-byte gates (1/256^8 for blind). cmplog/vpc clear both via two I2S substitutions; naive/value_profile expected ~0.
LITERAL_CHAIN_LEN=4: four sequential gates; blind search infeasible (1/256^16); has_axis still resolves via per-gate substitution.
LITERAL_CHAIN_LEN=8: eight sequential gates; lacks_axis=0 with certainty; has_axis time-to-trap grows but stays tractable, exercising the full literal pool.

screen._note: screen-tuning experiment (2026-05-24): override for the fast re-screen; honest grid stays in scan_values above
